mcqs.question: What is the parent class of JTextField in the Swing hierarchy?
mcqs.explanation: JTextField extends JTextComponent which is an abstract class. JTextComponent is the superclass for all Swing text components including JTextField, JTextArea, and JEditorPane.

mcqs.question: Which method is used to set the maximum number of characters in a JTextField?
mcqs.explanation: To limit input length, you need to set a DocumentFilter (like LengthFilter) on the document. setColumns() only sets the preferred column width for display, not the actual character limit.

mcqs.question: When is an ActionListener triggered on a JTextField?

options.text: When the text field gains focus
When the Enter key is pressed
When the text field loses focus

mcqs.explanation: ActionListener.actionPerformed() is invoked only when the user presses the Enter key while the text field has input focus. For real-time character detection, use DocumentListener instead.

mcqs.question: What does JTextField.setEditable(false) do?

options.text: Disables the component and grays it out
Prevents text modification but allows selection
Clears all text in the field

mcqs.explanation: setEditable(false) makes the text field read-only - users cannot modify text but can select, copy, and paste. Use setEnabled(false) to also visually disable the component.

mcqs.question: Which interface should be implemented to validate JTextField input in real-time?
mcqs.explanation: DocumentListener notifies you whenever the document is changed (inserted, removed, or modified). It provides insertUpdate(), removeUpdate(), and changedUpdate() methods for real-time validation.

mcqs.question: What is returned by JPasswordField.getPassword()?
mcqs.explanation: getPassword() returns a char array, not a String. This is for security reasons - char arrays can be cleared after use, whereas Strings remain in memory until garbage collected.

mcqs.question: How do you create a JTextField that accepts only numeric input?

options.text: Use a DocumentFilter that accepts only digits
Use KeyAdapter to filter non-digit keys

mcqs.explanation: The proper way is to use a DocumentFilter that only allows digit characters to be inserted. KeyAdapter approach has issues (paste, copy-paste can bypass it).

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of JTextField.addNotify()?

options.text: Called when the component is added to a visible hierarchy
To register for document change notifications

mcqs.explanation: addNotify() is a method called when the component is added to a visible GUI hierarchy. It's part of the Component hierarchy and is used for peer creation, not for notifications.

mcqs.question: Which method retrieves the underlying Document of a JTextField?
mcqs.explanation: getDocument() returns the Document interface that manages the text content. This allows programmatic access to text content and registration of DocumentListeners and DocumentFilters.

mcqs.question: What happens if you call setText(null) on a JTextField?
mcqs.explanation: setText(null) is safely handled and sets the text to an empty string. This behavior is inherited from JTextComponent and is well-defined.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between getText() and getSelectedText()?

options.text: getText() returns all text; getSelectedText() returns highlighted portion
getText() is deprecated in favor of getSelectedText()

mcqs.explanation: getText() returns the entire text content, while getSelectedText() returns only the currently selected (highlighted) portion. If no text is selected, getSelectedText() returns null.

mcqs.question: How do you implement input validation that prevents invalid characters from being entered?

options.text: Use KeyListener and consume invalid key events
Use DocumentFilter with insertString() and remove() methods

mcqs.explanation: DocumentFilter intercepts document modifications before they occur. The insertString() method allows you to validate and optionally reject or modify incoming text.

mcqs.question: What is the default number of columns when creating JTextField without arguments?
mcqs.explanation: The default constructor JTextField() creates a text field with 0 columns, meaning it uses the layout manager's default sizing. Always specify column count for predictable sizing.

mcqs.question: Which layout consideration is important for JTextField sizing?

options.text: setPreferredSize() is the primary sizing method

mcqs.explanation: setColumns(int) sets the preferred number of character columns, which the layout manager uses to calculate preferred width. This is the recommended way to size text fields consistently.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of copying JTextField's UI delegate?

options.text: To customize the visual appearance of the text field
To change the look and feel dynamically
BasicTextFieldUI handles all text rendering and editing

mcqs.explanation: BasicTextFieldUI (or corresponding UI delegate) handles all rendering and editing behavior. Customization is typically done through the Look and Feel or by overriding paintComponent().
